sections.paragraph:
κυνηγέσιον, ου, τό (s. two next entries; Eur., Hdt.+) animal hunt (usu. in pl., as e.g. OGI 529, 14; CIG 2511), instituted on some festival days by certain officials, e.g. in Smyrna by the Asiarchs. MPol 12:2. B. 190.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

usu. usu. = usually

  1. pl. = plural

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 575
